zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Braose於1330年出生在英國諾福爾可時，她的父親Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr24歲，母親Lady Agnes Eva de Ufford20歲。她和Sir William De Witchingham Knight至少有3個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1374年在她的家鄉享年44歲。

nameMeanings.name: Braose
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Eckhard, Gerhard, Manfred, Otto.
from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Ambrosius (see Ambrose ).

variants.name: Ambrose
meanings: English and Scottish: from the personal name Ambrose (French Ambroise, Latin Ambrosius, from Greek ambrosios ‘immortal, divine’), which was popular throughout Christendom in medieval Europe. Its popularity was due in part to the fame of Saint Ambrose (c. 340–397), one of the four Latin Fathers of the Church, the teacher of Saint Augustine. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Czech, Slovenian, and Croatian Ambrož (see Ambroz ), and also their derivatives, e.g. the Slovenian patronymic Ambrožič.
Irish: from Mac Ambróis ‘son of Ambrose’ (see 1 above); a West Munster name, which has also been Anglicized as McCambridge .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Braose was born in 1330, in Norfolk, England, her father, Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr, was 24 and her mother, Lady Agnes Eva de Ufford, was 20. She had at least 3 sons and 1 daughter with Sir William De Witchingham Knight. She died in 1374, in her hometown, at the age of 44.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Braose est née en 1330, à Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Lady Agnes Eva de Ufford, avait 20 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille avec Sir William De Witchingham Knight. Elle est décédée en 1374, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 44 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Braose 1330 in Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr 24 und ihre Mutter Lady Agnes Eva de Ufford 20. Sie hatte mit Sir William De Witchingham Knight mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1374 im Alter von 44 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Braose è nata il nel 1330 nel Norfolk, England, sua padre, Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr, aveva 24 anni e sua madre, Lady Agnes Eva de Ufford, aveva 20 anni. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia con Sir William De Witchingham Knight. Morì nel 1374, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 44 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Braose(이)가 1330년 영국 노르폴크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John de Braose - Lord of Stinton and Ludborough Sr(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Agnes Eva de Ufford(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 Sir William De Witchingham Knight(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1374년 그녀의 고향에서 44세의 나이로 사망했다.
